content: I'm at a crossroads with my journalism career. I just bought a house in Berlin and have been covering tech for 15 years now.
Congratulations on the house in Berlin! What's making you reconsider your journalism career at this point?
Well, I'm 56 and the renovation is taking longer than expected. Plus I'm wondering if I should pivot to something less demanding. I've been building mechanical keyboards as a hobby.
Mechanical keyboards sound fascinating. How did you get into that hobby?
I started during lockdown - found it oddly satisfying to build something with my hands after writing all day. I've also been doing more birdwatching since moving to Germany.
That's quite a range of interests! Have you considered how you might combine your tech journalism background with your hobbies?

I've thought about writing a book on the intersection of technology and craftsmanship. I'm also in a sci-fi book club that meets monthly - we often discuss how tech is portrayed in fiction versus reality.
A book sounds like an exciting project! What kind of sci-fi do you enjoy most?
Classic authors mostly - Asimov, Le Guin. I do the New York Times crossword puzzle every morning, though I'm still getting used to the German ones.
The crossword habit is impressive! Have you thought about freelance opportunities that might give you more flexibility while you renovate?
I have, but I'm worried about losing the stability of my current position. The house needs so much work - new electrical, plumbing, everything.
That does sound like a major project. How are you managing the renovation while working full-time?
I'm taking some vacation days to oversee the major work. My partner Alex has been incredibly patient through all of this. We're both wondering if I should just take a sabbatical.
A sabbatical could give you breathing room to think things through. What would you do with that time if you took it?
I'd finish the house renovations, maybe travel to some tech manufacturing hubs in Asia, and finally start that book. I just don't know if I can afford the career break.
Those sound like worthwhile goals. Have you discussed the sabbatical idea with your editors?
